c74object.name: tap.808.cymbal~
c74object.digest: TR-808-style cymbal voice, synthesized from the analog circuit
c74object.description: The tap.808.cymbal~ object is a circuit-informed model of the Roland TR-808 cymbal: the shared bank of six square oscillators, filtered through the hardware's ~3.4 and ~7.1 kHz voicings and split into strike, ring, and body components with separate decays. The tone knob balances strike against body (the hardware's level-ratio control); the decay knob sets the ring, spanning roughly 350 ms to 1.2 s overall like the original. Trigger with a signal rising edge (amplitude 0..1 = accent) or bang / trigger messages. The tolerance attribute spreads the oscillators per seed up to the real ~20% production tolerance — the documented reason no two 808s' cymbals sound alike.

inlet.digest: Trigger: a rising edge fires the voice; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent

method.name: signal
method.description: A rising edge triggers the cymbal, sample-accurately; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent.

method.name: bang
method.description: Trigger at full accent (from the scheduler).

method.name: trigger
method.description: Trigger with an accent value (0..1, default 1).

attribute.name: tone
attribute.description: 0 = all body (~3.4 kHz voicing), 1 = all strike (~7.1 kHz). Default 0.5.

attribute.name: decay
attribute.description: 0..1 (default 0.5); the hardware spans roughly 350 ms to 1.2 s overall.

attribute.name: tuning
attribute.digest: Circuit bend: bank pitch ratio (default 1 = stock)
attribute.description: Pitch ratio of the six-oscillator bank, 0.25..4.

attribute.name: tolerance
attribute.description: Spread of the six oscillator frequencies, 0..1: 1 is the full ~20% production tolerance. Deterministic per seed.

attribute.name: seed
attribute.digest: Unit identity for the tolerance spread
attribute.description: Seed (>= 1); each seed is a different unit off the line.
